content: [DECISION] Chose Clerk over Auth0 for Navi auth. Main reasons: native Next.js middleware support, built-in org/role primitives that map well to SPV fund-manager vs LP roles, and pricing is better under 10k MAU. Marcus and I evaluated both over two days. Auth0 Actions felt over-engineered for our needs.

[FACT] Clerk session tokens are JWTs with a default TTL of 60 seconds for the short-lived token, backed by a long-lived session (7 days default). The short-lived token is auto-refreshed by the Clerk frontend SDK. We can customize both values in the Clerk dashboard under Sessions.

[PLAN] For SOC2 audit prep, we need to document: (1) session timeout policy, (2) MFA enforcement for fund managers, (3) audit log retention for auth events. Priya will own the controls spreadsheet. Target completion: March 15. Clerk provides an audit log API we can pipe into Datadog.

[MEETING] Standup 2024-02-06. Marcus demoed Clerk webhook integration for syncing user creation events to our Postgres users table. Discussed edge case: if webhook fails, user exists in Clerk but not in our DB. Agreed to add a reconciliation cron job that runs every 15 minutes.

[DECISION] Going with magic-link as the primary auth method instead of passwords. LPs are infrequent users (log in maybe once a quarter to check distributions), so password fatigue is real. Magic link reduces support tickets. Fund managers get magic-link + optional TOTP MFA.

[CORRECTION] Previously assumed Clerk's `orgRole` claim would be available in the middleware JWT by default. It's not — you have to enable 'Include organization claims' in the JWT template settings. Marcus spent 3 hours debugging before finding this. Updated the setup doc in Notion.

[HANDOFF] Priya is taking over the Clerk webhook → Postgres sync from Marcus while he's on PTO Feb 12-19. Key files: /lib/clerk-webhooks.ts and /app/api/webhooks/clerk/route.ts. The Svix signing secret is in 1Password vault 'Navi Staging'.

[COMPLETION] Finished implementing role-based middleware guards. Fund managers (org:admin) can access /dashboard/manage, LPs (org:member) can only access /dashboard/portfolio. Unauthorized access returns 403 and redirects to /unauthorized. Merged in PR #147.

[FACT] Clerk charges $0.02/MAU after the free tier of 10,000 MAU. Auth0 free tier caps at 7,500 MAU and jumps to $23/mo for the Essentials plan. For Navi's projected 2,000 users in year one, both are effectively free, but Clerk scales cheaper up to ~50k.

[PLAN] Need to implement session revocation flow for when an LP is removed from an SPV. Steps: (1) fund manager removes LP in our app, (2) we call Clerk API to revoke all sessions for that user in the org, (3) fire webhook to clean up DB permissions. Target: sprint 14.

[MEETING] Call with Clerk solutions engineer (Dana) on 2024-02-09. Confirmed that Clerk supports custom session claims via JWT templates. We can embed `spv_ids` array in the token so middleware can gate access per-SPV without a DB call. She sent example config.

[DECISION] Setting session TTL to 24 hours for fund managers and 72 hours for LPs. Rationale: fund managers handle sensitive operations (capital calls, distributions) so shorter sessions reduce risk. LPs mostly view read-only data. Configurable per-role via Clerk JWT template conditions.

[CORRECTION] The reconciliation cron job (episode from standup 2/6) was hitting Clerk's rate limit of 20 req/s when checking all users. Switched to using Clerk's `users.getUserList` with pagination and added a 100ms delay between pages. Fixed in PR #153.

[COMPLETION] SOC2 auth controls documentation finished. Priya submitted the controls spreadsheet covering session management, MFA policy, and audit log retention (90 days in Datadog). Auditor (Vanta) accepted the evidence package on March 12, three days ahead of deadline.

[FACT] NextAuth was briefly considered but ruled out early. It requires managing our own session store and doesn't provide a hosted UI. Clerk's prebuilt <SignIn /> component saved us roughly 2 weeks of frontend work for login, signup, MFA, and org-switching flows.

[DECISION] Using Stripe Connect Custom accounts for LP capital calls instead of Standard accounts. Custom gives us full control over payout timing, which matters for quarterly carry distributions. Marcus confirmed legal is fine with this approach as long as we handle 1099 reporting ourselves.

[FACT] Stripe test-mode webhooks use a separate signing secret from live-mode. The test secret is whsec_test_7kPl... stored in 1Password under 'Navi Stripe Dev'. Live secret rotates quarterly per Marcus's security policy. Both are injected via Doppler env vars in staging/prod.

[PLAN] K-1 report generation pipeline: 1) Pull carry distributions from Stripe payouts table, 2) Join with LP entity data from Postgres, 3) Generate PDFs via LaTeX template, 4) Upload to S3 with presigned URLs, 5) Email LPs via SendGrid. Target: ready by Feb 15 for 2024 tax year.

[MEETING] Sync with Priya and Derek on idempotency key strategy. Decided keys will be formatted as `navi_{fund_id}_{call_number}_{lp_id}` to prevent duplicate capital call charges. Priya raised edge case: what if an LP updates their bank account mid-call? We'll void and re-issue with a new suffix `-v2`.

[CORRECTION] The webhook endpoint was returning 200 even on failed DB writes, causing Stripe to think events were processed. Fixed by wrapping the handler in a transaction and only returning 200 after commit. Derek caught this when $42K in test-mode contributions weren't showing in the dashboard.

[COMPLETION] Finished the LP contribution flow end-to-end in test mode. ACH debits trigger `payment_intent.succeeded` webhook → updates `contributions` table → sends confirmation email via SendGrid. Tested with 12 simulated LPs across Fund II. Average settlement time in test: instant (live will be 3-5 business days).

[DECISION] Carry waterfall calculation runs nightly as a cron job in the `navi-calc` service, not in real-time. GP carry is 20% over 8% preferred return. We store intermediate waterfall state in a `carry_ledger` table so auditors can trace every distribution back to its source transaction.

[HANDOFF] Handing the Stripe Connect onboarding UI to Priya. She'll build the React form that collects LP banking details and submits to our `/api/connect/create-account` endpoint. I've documented the required fields in Notion under Navi > Stripe Integration. Test API key is already in her Doppler config.

[FACT] Stripe charges 0.8% per ACH debit with a $5 cap per transaction. For our average LP contribution of $250K, that's $5 per call. Fund II has 34 LPs, so each capital call round costs ~$170 in Stripe fees. Marcus approved this as immaterial relative to fund size ($85M).

[PLAN] Need to implement retry logic for failed ACH debits. Stripe auto-retries once, but we want a manual retry flow in the GP admin dashboard. Derek will add a `retry_contribution` endpoint that generates a new PaymentIntent with the same idempotency key plus `-retry-{n}` suffix. Target: next sprint.

[MEETING] Call with Stripe solutions engineer (Anika) about handling carry distributions to 34 LPs simultaneously. She recommended using Stripe's batch payout API with a 500ms delay between requests to avoid rate limits. Also suggested we use `transfer_group` to link related payouts for reconciliation.

[DECISION] K-1 reports will be generated using a LaTeX template rather than a React-PDF approach. Derek benchmarked both: LaTeX produces cleaner output and handles the multi-page schedules better. We'll run `pdflatex` in a Lambda function with a custom layer that includes TeX Live minimal.

[CORRECTION] Carry calculation was using committed capital instead of contributed capital for the preferred return hurdle. This overstated the hurdle by ~$3.2M for Fund II. Fixed the query in `carry_ledger.sql` to join on `contributions.status = 'settled'` instead of `commitments.amount`. Priya verified with spreadsheet.

[COMPLETION] GP carry distribution for Fund I Q3 2024 executed successfully. 22 LPs received payouts totaling $1.87M via Stripe Connect. All `transfer_group` IDs match ledger entries. Reconciliation report uploaded to S3 at `navi-reports/fund1/q3-2024/carry-recon.csv`. Marcus signed off.

[HANDOFF] Passing webhook monitoring to Derek. Set up a Datadog dashboard 'Navi Webhooks' tracking event volume, processing latency, and failure rate. Alert fires if failure rate exceeds 2% over 5 minutes. Runbook is in Notion under Navi > Ops > Webhook Failures. PagerDuty rotation starts Monday.

[DECISION] We'll use a temporal table pattern for deals: valid_from and valid_to columns on deals and term_sheets tables. valid_to defaults to 'infinity'::timestamptz. This lets us reconstruct deal state at any point in time without losing history. Marcus proposed this after the audit issue in Q1.

[FACT] The deals table has columns: id (uuid PK), company_name (text NOT NULL), stage (text CHECK IN ('sourced','diligence','term_sheet','closed','passed')), lead_partner (text), amount_usd (numeric(15,2)), valid_from (timestamptz), valid_to (timestamptz DEFAULT 'infinity').

[PLAN] Migration strategy for the LP allocation table: 1) Create lp_allocations_v2 with new constraints. 2) Backfill from lp_allocations using a Python script (scripts/backfill_alloc.py). 3) Rename tables in a single transaction. 4) Drop old table after 2-week soak period. Priya owns the backfill script.

[MEETING] Sync with Priya and Marcus on 2024-06-03. Agreed that term_sheets needs a UNIQUE constraint on (deal_id, version, valid_to) to prevent duplicate active versions. Marcus will write the migration. Target: deploy by Friday EOD.

[CORRECTION] The CHECK constraint on deals.stage was missing 'passed' as a valid value. Deals marked as passed were failing inserts since migration 027. Hotfixed in migration 028_add_passed_stage.sql, deployed 2024-05-29 at 14:22 UTC.

[COMPLETION] Migration 029_lp_allocation_v2.sql is live in production. New lp_allocations table includes fund_id (uuid FK to funds), deal_id (uuid FK to deals), lp_id (uuid FK to lps), committed_usd (numeric(15,2)), and valid_from/valid_to temporal columns. Zero downtime achieved.

[HANDOFF] Marcus is on PTO June 10-21. Priya takes over remaining constraint updates on term_sheets. Open items: add NOT NULL on term_sheets.valuation_cap, add index on (deal_id, valid_to) for the common query pattern. Notes in Linear ticket NAV-412.

[FACT] The term_sheets table schema: id (uuid PK), deal_id (uuid FK deals), version (int NOT NULL), valuation_cap (numeric(15,2)), discount_pct (numeric(5,2)), pro_rata (boolean DEFAULT false), signed_at (timestamptz), valid_from (timestamptz), valid_to (timestamptz DEFAULT 'infinity').

[DECISION] LP allocation percentages will be stored as committed_usd rather than percentage of fund. Reason: percentages shift as fund size changes during fundraising. Absolute dollar amounts are the source of truth per the fund admin (Carta) export format.

[PLAN] Next sprint (June 17): build a materialized view mv_active_deals that filters WHERE valid_to = 'infinity'. Refresh nightly via pg_cron at 02:00 UTC. This powers the dashboard without hitting the temporal query overhead. Estimated 40x speedup on the main pipeline query.

[MEETING] Call with fund admin (Carta) on 2024-06-05. They confirmed LP commitment data exports as CSV with columns: lp_name, fund, commitment_amount, effective_date. We'll map effective_date to valid_from. No valid_to in their export—we default to 'infinity' and close manually on redemption.

[CORRECTION] The backfill script (scripts/backfill_alloc.py) had a bug: it was setting valid_from to NULL for 137 rows where the original created_at was missing. Fixed by defaulting to the fund inception date (2023-01-15). Priya pushed fix in commit a3f7c91.

[FACT] Postgres version is 15.4 on RDS (db.r6g.xlarge). We use pgcrypto for uuid generation (gen_random_uuid()). Connection pooling via PgBouncer at max 80 connections. All migrations managed through sqitch with plan file in db/sqitch.plan.

[DECISION] Chose sqitch over Flyway for migration management. Reason: sqitch supports revert scripts natively and the team already has experience from the Atlas project. Marcus set up the initial sqitch project structure in db/ directory.

[COMPLETION] Index idx_term_sheets_deal_valid on term_sheets(deal_id, valid_to) deployed in migration 030. Query time for fetching active term sheet by deal dropped from 340ms to 8ms on the 1.2M row staging dataset. Priya verified via EXPLAIN ANALYZE.

[DECISION] Chose Sanity v3 over Contentful for CMS. Sanity's GROQ query language gives us more flexibility for the investor dashboard content models, and the real-time collaboration features will help the marketing team. Marcus signed off on the $499/mo growth plan.

[PLAN] Sprint 1 (Oct 2-13): Scaffold Next.js 14 app router, configure Sanity studio, set up Vercel project with preview deployments. Sprint 2 (Oct 16-27): Hero section, navigation, mobile-first responsive grid. Sprint 3: Investor dashboard pages and auth integration.

[FACT] Figma designs from Lena use the new Foundry brand palette: primary #1A2B4A (deep navy), secondary #E8A838 (amber), neutral grays. Typography is Inter for body, Fraunces for display headings. Mobile breakpoints at 375px, 768px, 1024px, 1440px.

[MEETING] Kickoff with stakeholders Oct 1. Attendees: Marcus (PM), Lena (design), Raj (backend), Sofia (frontend), Derek (Foundry CEO). Derek emphasized that LP investors need quarterly NAV charts and capital call history visible on mobile. Target launch: Nov 15.

[DECISION] Going with NextAuth.js + custom credentials provider for LP portal auth instead of Clerk. Raj pointed out we need to integrate with Foundry's existing investor identity database in Postgres. Clerk's user sync would add unnecessary complexity.

[COMPLETION] Sofia finished the responsive navigation component with mobile hamburger menu, slide-out drawer, and scroll-lock behavior. Tested on iOS Safari, Chrome Android, and desktop browsers. PR #34 merged to main. Lighthouse mobile score: 96.

[HANDOFF] Raj handed the Sanity schema definitions to Sofia. Includes document types: fundOverview, quarterlyReport, capitalCall, teamMember, pressRelease. All schemas have draft/published workflow configured. Studio deployed at foundry-studio.sanity.studio.

[FACT] The investor dashboard pulls data from two sources: Sanity CMS for narrative content (fund letters, announcements) and a REST API at api.foundry.capital/v2 for financial data (NAV, IRR, cash flows). API uses bearer token auth with 15-min expiry JWTs.

[CORRECTION] The Recharts library was causing hydration mismatches in Next.js app router due to SSR. Switched to using dynamic imports with ssr: false for all chart components. Also had to wrap the NAV time-series chart in a Suspense boundary. Fixed in PR #52.

[PLAN] SEO strategy: generate static pages for public marketing content at build time using generateStaticParams. Investor dashboard routes behind auth will be fully client-rendered. Sitemap auto-generated from Sanity slugs via next-sitemap. Target: 50+ indexed pages by launch.

[MEETING] Design review Oct 20 with Lena and Derek. Derek requested adding a fund comparison table to the LP dashboard — side-by-side view of Fund I, Fund II, and Fund III performance. Lena will deliver updated Figma by Oct 23. Sofia estimated 3 days for implementation.

[DECISION] Deploying to Vercel Pro ($20/seat) rather than self-hosting on AWS. Preview deployments per PR are critical for marketing team reviews. ISR revalidation set to 60 seconds for fund pages. Edge middleware handles geo-redirect for EU compliance pages.

[COMPLETION] Raj finished the quarterly report PDF generation pipeline. Uses @react-pdf/renderer to create branded LP reports from Sanity data + API financials. PDFs cached in Vercel Blob storage. Investors can download from dashboard. Tested with Q3 2024 data.

[HANDOFF] Sofia handed Sanity Studio training docs to the Foundry marketing team (Claire and Tom). Covers creating blog posts, editing team bios, publishing press releases. Recorded a 20-min Loom walkthrough. They'll manage content independently post-launch.

[CORRECTION] Discovered that the capital call history table was showing amounts in cents instead of dollars — the API returns integer cents. Added a formatCurrency utility that divides by 100 and applies Intl.NumberFormat('en-US', {style: 'currency', currency: 'USD'}). Hotfix PR #61.

[DECISION] Chose SendGrid over Mailgun for the Foundry newsletter stack. Pricing is better at our projected 25k subscriber tier, and their v3 API has native support for dynamic templates which we need for the weekly macro briefing layout.
[FACT] SendGrid API key is stored in Vault under secret/foundry/sendgrid. The key has Mail Send and Marketing Campaigns scopes only — no admin access. Rotated quarterly per infosec policy.

[PLAN] Subscriber segmentation will use three tiers: free (macro briefing only), pro (macro + sector deep-dives), and institutional (custom data attachments). Segments are synced nightly from Postgres via a cron job running on the foundry-worker k8s deployment.

[COMPLETION] DKIM and SPF records for foundry-mail.io are live. DKIM key is 2048-bit, selector is sg1. SPF includes include:sendgrid.net. Verified in SendGrid dashboard and confirmed DMARC alignment with dmarcian. Rachel signed off.

[MEETING] Sync with Marcus and Priya on unsubscribe flow. Agreed: one-click unsubscribe header per RFC 8058, plus a preference center page at foundry-mail.io/preferences. Marcus will handle the backend webhook, Priya owns the React preference center UI.

[HANDOFF] Handing the SendGrid event webhook integration to Marcus. Events (delivered, opened, bounced, spam_report) POST to /api/webhooks/sendgrid on the foundry-api service. Schema doc is in Notion under Foundry > Integrations > SendGrid Events.

[CORRECTION] The weekly macro briefing was going out at 6:00 AM UTC, but analytics showed best open rates at 10:30 AM ET. Changed the cron schedule from '0 6 * * 1' to '30 14 * * 1' (UTC). Open rate jumped from 22% to 31% after two weeks.

[FACT] SendGrid dynamic template ID for the weekly macro briefing is d-8a3f29bc41e7. It uses Handlebars syntax. Variables: {{subscriber_name}}, {{briefing_date}}, {{macro_summary}}, {{chart_url}}, {{unsubscribe_link}}.

[DECISION] Will store unsubscribe events in a dedicated `email_suppressions` table rather than a boolean flag on the subscribers table. This preserves history and lets us track re-subscriptions for compliance audits. Migration 20240312_create_suppressions.sql is merged.

[PLAN] Next sprint: implement bounce handling. Hard bounces auto-suppress after 1 occurrence. Soft bounces retry 3 times over 72 hours, then suppress. Marcus is writing the bounce processor service; target completion April 5.

[MEETING] Call with SendGrid TAM (Jessica Liu) about dedicated IP warm-up plan. She recommended starting at 500 emails/day, ramping 2x every 3 days. Full volume (~25k) reachable in about 18 days. She'll send us the warm-up schedule doc.

[COMPLETION] Preference center UI is deployed at foundry-mail.io/preferences. Subscribers can toggle macro briefing, sector deep-dives, and event invitations independently. Priya finished it two days ahead of schedule. Lighthouse score 97.

[FACT] Current subscriber counts as of March 20: free tier 18,420, pro tier 4,310, institutional 87. Total active 22,817. Suppressed (bounces + unsubscribes) 1,204. Data pulled from the foundry analytics Metabase dashboard.

[HANDOFF] Passing DMARC monitoring to the infosec team (lead: Rachel Nguyen). Weekly aggregate reports go to dmarc-reports@foundry-mail.io. Forensic reports are off for now due to PII concerns. Rachel has access to the dmarcian dashboard.

[CORRECTION] The unsubscribe webhook was silently failing because the endpoint expected Content-Type application/json but SendGrid sends application/x-www-form-urlencoded for some event types. Marcus patched the middleware to accept both. Deployed hotfix v1.4.2.

[DECISION] Chose upsert (INSERT ... ON CONFLICT) over delete-then-insert for dedup in the market_data_raw table. Marcus benchmarked both approaches on 12M rows and upsert was 3.2x faster with fewer lock contention issues on the Postgres 15 instance.

[PLAN] Backfill strategy for Catalyst: run historical ingestion DAGs in 30-day chunks going back to 2019-01-01. Each chunk writes to a staging table, dedup runs, then merge into market_data_raw. Estimated 847M rows total. Target completion: end of sprint 14.

[FACT] The market_data_raw table has a composite unique index on (symbol, exchange, ts_utc). Partitioned by month on ts_utc using Postgres native declarative partitioning. Current partition count is 62.

[MEETING] Standup with Marcus, Priya, and Devon. Discussed Airflow worker memory limits—current 4GB ceiling causes OOM on the equity_daily_ingest task when processing NASDAQ full-day snapshots. Priya will test bumping to 8GB on the k8s executor.

[CORRECTION] The FX data source (Refinitiv) timestamps were assumed UTC but are actually ET. Marcus found a 5-hour offset in backfilled FX rows from 2019-2021. We need to reprocess ~38M rows. Filed JIRA CAT-412.

[COMPLETION] Backfill for equity data 2019-01-01 through 2022-12-31 finished. 614M rows loaded into market_data_raw. Checksums validated against source parquet files. No duplicates detected in post-load audit.

[HANDOFF] Devon handed off the Airflow DAG definitions (catalyst_ingest_daily, catalyst_backfill_chunk) to Priya. DAGs live in airflow/dags/catalyst/. Connections configured: pg_catalyst_prod, s3_market_landing. Slack channel #catalyst-ops for alerts.

[DECISION] Using S3 as the landing zone for raw market data files before ingestion. Considered Azure Blob but our existing infra is AWS. Landing bucket: s3://catalyst-market-landing/. Lifecycle policy deletes files after 90 days.

[FACT] Airflow version is 2.7.3 running on Kubernetes executor in EKS cluster us-east-1. Scheduler has 2 replicas. DAG bag parse interval set to 30s. All Catalyst DAGs use pool 'catalyst_pg_pool' limited to 6 concurrent slots.

[PLAN] After backfill completes, implement a daily reconciliation DAG (catalyst_recon_daily) that compares row counts and min/max timestamps between source parquet manifests and Postgres partitions. Alert via PagerDuty if drift > 0.01%.

[MEETING] Architecture review with Devon, Marcus, and team lead Sandra. Decided against adding Kafka between S3 and Postgres for now—throughput needs are ~50k rows/sec peak which Postgres handles fine. Revisit if we add real-time streaming in Q3.

[CORRECTION] The catalyst_backfill_chunk DAG had a fencepost error: chunks for month boundaries were skipping the last day. E.g., January chunk ended at 2019-01-30 instead of 2019-01-31. Fixed by Devon in commit a3f7c21. Affected 48 monthly chunks.

[DECISION] Partition pruning requires queries to include ts_utc in WHERE clause. Added a check constraint wrapper function (require_ts_filter) as a Postgres function invoked by the application layer. Priya implemented it in the SQLAlchemy models.
[COMPLETION] FX backfill reprocessing (CAT-412) done. All 38M rows corrected from ET to UTC. Verified by spot-checking 500 random rows against Bloomberg terminal timestamps. Marcus signed off.

[HANDOFF] Sandra is taking over Catalyst ops ownership while Marcus is on leave (Dec 15 – Jan 5). Runbook at confluence/catalyst/runbook.md covers partition maintenance, DAG failure triage, and PagerDuty escalation paths.

[DECISION] Chose Claude Sonnet 3.5 for the main summarization pass instead of Opus. Latency dropped from 12s to 3.8s per transcript, and summary quality delta was negligible on our 50-transcript eval set. Opus reserved for complex multi-quarter comparison tasks only.

[FACT] Neo4j Aura instance is on the Professional tier, us-east-1. Graph schema: (:Company)-[:REPORTED]->(:Earnings {quarter, year, revenue, eps})-[:MENTIONED]->(:Topic). ~14k nodes after loading S&P 500 transcripts from 2022-2024.

[PLAN] Sprint 7 goals: (1) wire up Cypher-based RAG retrieval so the agent can pull related quarters before generating a summary card, (2) add streaming SSE endpoint for card generation, (3) Priya to build the React card preview component.

[MEETING] Sync with Raj and Priya on 2024-06-03. Agreed that summary cards should include: revenue delta %, EPS beat/miss, top 3 management themes, and a sentiment score. Raj will own the prompt template; Priya handles the frontend card layout in Figma first.

[CORRECTION] The Cypher query for fetching related quarters was using undirected relationships, pulling in garbage nodes. Fixed by switching to directed pattern (:Company)-[:REPORTED]->(:Earnings) and adding a WHERE clause on year >= $targetYear - 2.

[DECISION] Embedding model for transcript chunk retrieval: went with text-embedding-3-large (3072 dims) over text-embedding-3-small. Recall@10 improved from 0.71 to 0.84 on our test set of 200 analyst questions. Stored in Neo4j vector index.

[COMPLETION] Finished the earnings transcript ingestion pipeline. PDFs are parsed with PyMuPDF, chunked at 512 tokens with 64-token overlap, embedded, and upserted into Neo4j. Processed 2,847 transcripts in 4h 12m on a single c5.2xlarge.

[HANDOFF] Handing the prompt engineering work to Raj. Current prompt v3 is in prompts/summary_card_v3.txt. Known issue: the model sometimes hallucinates EPS numbers when the transcript is ambiguous. Raj should cross-check against the structured Earnings node data.

[FACT] Claude Sonnet 3.5 context window is 200k tokens. Typical earnings transcript is 8k-15k tokens after cleaning. We concatenate up to 4 prior quarter summaries (~600 tokens each) as RAG context, staying well under limits.

[PLAN] Next milestone: add a comparison mode where the agent generates a side-by-side card for two companies in the same sector. Requires a new Cypher query to fetch sector peers via (:Company)-[:IN_SECTOR]->(:Sector) and a new prompt template.

[MEETING] Demo to stakeholders on 2024-06-14. Marcus from product asked for a confidence score on each summary card. We proposed using the log-probs from the Sonnet output but Claude API doesn't expose log-probs. Will use a separate validation pass with Haiku instead.

[DECISION] For the validation/confidence pass, chose Claude Haiku over a fine-tuned classifier. Haiku checks the summary card against raw transcript excerpts and returns a JSON with per-field confidence. Cost is ~$0.002 per card, acceptable at our volume.

[CORRECTION] Sentiment score was skewing positive because the prompt asked the model to rate 'overall tone' and earnings calls are inherently optimistic. Revised prompt to focus on 'tone relative to prior quarter guidance' which gave much better variance across the dataset.

[COMPLETION] Streaming SSE endpoint for card generation is live at /api/v1/cards/stream. P95 latency is 4.2s to first token. Tested with 100 concurrent connections on staging; no dropped frames. Priya's React component consumes the stream correctly.

[HANDOFF] Transferring Neo4j admin responsibilities to Dani. Credentials are in 1Password vault 'Catalyst Infra'. Backup schedule is daily at 02:00 UTC via neo4j-admin dump to S3 bucket catalyst-neo4j-backups. Runbook is in docs/neo4j-ops.md.
[DECISION] Chose SvelteKit over Next.js for the Combinator MVP. Marcus argued SSR performance was comparable, and the team has more Svelte experience. Vote was 4-1 in favor during Monday standup.

[PLAN] Beta rollout in three phases: Phase 1 (May 12) — 20 internal users from the strategy team. Phase 2 (May 26) — 100 users from the Discord waitlist. Phase 3 (June 9) — open beta with landing page signup.
[FACT] The strategy mixing engine uses a weighted DAG where each node represents a tactic. Edge weights are derived from historical win-rate data stored in the combinator_edges table in Postgres.
[MEETING] Sync with Priya and Leo on May 3. Agreed the drag-and-drop strategy builder is the hero feature for launch. Leo will own the canvas component. Priya handles the constraint validation logic.

[DECISION] Hosting on Fly.io instead of Vercel. SvelteKit adapter-node gives us more control over WebSocket connections needed for real-time collaboration on strategy boards. Marcus set up the fly.toml config.
[COMPLETION] Leo finished the drag-and-drop canvas component using svelte-dnd-action. Supports reordering, grouping, and snapping to grid. Merged PR #47 on May 8. Needs accessibility pass still.

[HANDOFF] Priya handed off the constraint validation module to Tomás before her PTO (May 15-22). All specs are in the /docs/constraints folder. Unit tests cover 83% of edge cases. Tomás should focus on cycle detection.

[FACT] The combinator_edges table currently holds 14,200 rows of historical tactic pair data scraped from public tournament logs. Data pipeline runs nightly via a cron job on Fly.io machine 'worker-1'.

[CORRECTION] Previous assumption that we could use SSE for real-time sync was wrong. Strategy boards require bidirectional updates. Switched to WebSocket via socket.io after Marcus benchmarked latency at <40ms on Fly.io.
[PLAN] Landing page for open beta will be built with Tailwind + static SvelteKit route. Copy drafted by Dana. Target: collect 500 signups before June 9 launch. UTM tracking via Plausible analytics.
[DECISION] Using Drizzle ORM instead of Prisma. Tomás benchmarked both — Drizzle had 30% faster cold-start queries on Fly.io. Migration files stored in /db/migrations.

[MEETING] May 10 design review with Dana and Leo. Finalized the color palette: dark mode primary #1A1A2E, accent #E94560. Strategy node cards use rounded-lg with subtle drop shadows. Figma link updated in Notion.
[COMPLETION] Tomás finished cycle detection in the constraint validator. Uses Kahn's algorithm on the tactic DAG. All 47 unit tests passing. Merged PR #52 on May 18.

[HANDOFF] Marcus handed WebSocket infra ownership to Leo for the duration of the beta. Runbook is in /docs/ws-ops.md. Key thing: Fly.io machines need at least 512MB RAM or socket connections drop under load.

[CORRECTION] The Discord waitlist actually has 312 users, not 200 as reported in the May 3 meeting. Dana recounted after deduplicating emails. Phase 2 cohort size adjusted to 150 to account for higher interest.

[DECISION] Chose Neo4j 5.15 over ArangoDB for the graph layer. Neo4j's Cypher queries map cleanly to our room/hall/wing hierarchy, and Marcus confirmed the team already has Neo4j operational experience from the Athena project.

[FACT] The 21-type classifier maps every incoming memory to exactly one of: DECISION, PLAN, FACT, CORRECTION, MEETING, COMPLETION, HANDOFF, INSIGHT, QUESTION, OBSERVATION, PREFERENCE, CONSTRAINT, DEPENDENCY, RISK, MILESTONE, CONTEXT, PERSONA, TOOL, METRIC, HYPOTHESIS, REFLECTION.

[PLAN] Compaction cron runs nightly at 03:00 UTC. It merges duplicate or near-duplicate episodes (cosine similarity > 0.92 in ChromaDB) and archives stale nodes older than 90 days into a cold Neo4j database on the r6g.xlarge instance.

[MEETING] Sync with Priya and Dan on 2024-11-12. Agreed composite scoring formula: 0.4*recency + 0.3*relevance + 0.2*importance + 0.1*access_frequency. Dan will implement the scoring module in Python by Friday.

[COMPLETION] ChromaDB collection 'jarvis_episodes' is live in production. Indexed 14,200 episodes with sentence-transformer embeddings (all-MiniLM-L6-v2). Average query latency is 38ms at p95 on 3 shards.

[HANDOFF] Marcus handed off the Neo4j schema migration scripts to Priya. Migration v3 adds room_id, hall_id, and wing_id properties to all EpisodeNode entities. Priya to run migration on staging by Wednesday.

[CORRECTION] Previous assumption that group_id was optional was wrong. Every episode must have a group_id; the system group is the default. OpenClaw queries without group_id were silently returning empty results. Fixed in commit a4f82c1.

[DECISION] Room/hall/wing metadata uses a 3-tier namespace: wing is the broadest scope (e.g., 'engineering'), hall is a project (e.g., 'jarvis-memory'), room is a session or thread. This mirrors spatial memory palace metaphors from the design doc.

[FACT] The composite score is stored as a float32 on each Neo4j node under the property 'composite_score'. Scores range 0.0-1.0. Nodes below 0.15 are candidates for compaction. The score is recalculated on every read and every nightly cron pass.

[PLAN] Next sprint: integrate OpenClaw's tool-use traces as TOOL-type episodes. Each tool invocation gets its own episode with input hash, output summary, and latency. Target: capture 95% of tool calls with < 5ms overhead per call.

[MEETING] Architecture review 2024-11-19 with Dan, Marcus, and Lena. Lena raised concern about ChromaDB's lack of ACID guarantees. Decision: wrap writes in a two-phase commit—Neo4j first, ChromaDB second, with a rollback queue in Redis for failures.

[COMPLETION] The 21-type classifier (fine-tuned DistilBERT, 89.3% accuracy on held-out set) deployed to prod behind the /classify endpoint. Inference time ~12ms per episode. Fallback to keyword heuristics if model service is down.

[HANDOFF] Dan completed the composite scoring module and handed it to Lena for integration testing. Test suite covers 47 scenarios including edge cases for zero-access episodes and freshly created nodes with no recency decay.

[CORRECTION] The compaction cron was merging episodes across different group_ids due to a missing WHERE clause in the Cypher query. Priya patched it in migration v4. Only 23 cross-group merges occurred before detection; all were manually reversed.

[FACT] Neo4j indexes: composite index on (group_id, wing_id, hall_id) for scoped queries, full-text index on episode body for keyword fallback, and a point-in-time index on created_at for recency calculations. Total index size ~1.2GB.

[DECISION] Chose Mac Mini M2 (16GB) as the always-on host for OpenClaw. The low power draw (~10W idle) makes it viable as a 24/7 agent runner without racking up electricity costs. Marcus approved the $799 purchase on 2024-03-11.

[FACT] Tailscale node name is `claw-mini.tail1234.ts.net`. IP is 100.78.42.15. The Mac Mini sits behind a double-NAT at the office, so Tailscale is the only reliable way to reach it from outside. MagicDNS is enabled.

[PLAN] Bootstrap sequence for OpenClaw agent: 1) Install Homebrew + Node 20 LTS. 2) Clone openclaw repo. 3) Copy SOUL.md to ~/.openclaw/SOUL.md. 4) Register MCP tool surface endpoints. 5) Configure launchd plist for auto-start. 6) Verify Tailscale connectivity from Jenna's laptop.

[COMPLETION] launchd plist `com.openclaw.agent.plist` is installed in ~/Library/LaunchAgents and loads on login. Tested reboot cycle — agent comes back within 8 seconds of desktop session start. Logs go to /var/log/openclaw/agent.log with logrotate via newsyslog.conf.

[DECISION] Session handoff protocol: when the agent detects token expiry or context overflow, it serializes current state to ~/.openclaw/handoff.json, writes a summary to the session log, and spawns a fresh session that reads the handoff file on init. No human intervention needed.

[MEETING] Sync with Marcus and Jenna on 2024-03-18. Agreed that SOUL.md should be the single source of truth for agent personality and constraints. Marcus wants a git-tracked version in the repo root, with a deploy hook that copies it to the Mini on push.

[FACT] MCP tool surface currently exposes 7 tools: file_read, file_write, shell_exec, browser_fetch, memory_store, memory_query, and calendar_check. Each tool has a 30-second timeout enforced by the MCP server process running on port 7832.

[CORRECTION] The launchd plist originally used RunAtLoad=true with KeepAlive=true, but this caused duplicate agent processes after wake-from-sleep. Fixed by switching to KeepAlive with SuccessfulExit=false only, so launchd only restarts on crashes, not on wake events.

[HANDOFF] Jenna is taking over the Tailscale ACL config from Marcus. Current ACLs restrict the Mini to accept connections only from the `engineering` group tag. Jenna needs to add the `ops` group so the monitoring dashboard at grafana.tail1234.ts.net can scrape metrics.

[PLAN] Cron schedule via launchd for maintenance tasks: daily at 03:00 — rotate logs and prune handoff files older than 7 days. Weekly Sunday 05:00 — pull latest SOUL.md from main branch. Monthly 1st at 06:00 — run brew update && brew upgrade on all dependencies.

[FACT] SOUL.md v2.3 specifies: agent must never execute destructive shell commands (rm -rf, mkfs, etc.) without explicit human confirmation via the approval_request MCP tool. This constraint is enforced both in SOUL.md parsing and as a shell_exec pre-hook.

[DECISION] Chose newsyslog over logrotate for log management since newsyslog is native to macOS. Config entry added to /etc/newsyslog.d/openclaw.conf: rotate at 10MB, keep 5 compressed archives, signal the agent process with SIGUSR1 after rotation.

[COMPLETION] End-to-end session handoff test passed. Simulated context overflow at 120k tokens — agent wrote handoff.json (14KB), new session loaded it in 1.2s, and resumed the prior task (dependency audit) without losing tracked state. Marcus signed off on the implementation.

[CORRECTION] memory_store MCP tool was writing to /tmp/openclaw_memory.db, which gets wiped on reboot. Moved the SQLite database to ~/.openclaw/memory.db and updated the MCP server config. Verified data persists across restarts now.

[HANDOFF] Handing off to night-shift agent session. Current state: PR #247 (add calendar_check tool) is open, CI green, awaiting Jenna's review. Memory DB has 342 entries. No pending approval_requests. Tailscale connection stable, uptime 14 days.
